commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an internal streaming byte guard, wraps `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` SSE reads with a 16 MiB cap, exports the iterator for tests, and adds a regression test.

PR surface: Source +107, Tests +37. Total +144 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. as a source-reproducible path: current main appends Content-Length-less SSE chunks into an unbounded buffer in `iterateSseMessages`. I did not execute a current-main repro in this read-only pass, but the PR body includes after-fix local-server proof for the same attack shape.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance or tuning of the 16 MiB compatibility boundary, plus normal CI gating, not an automated repair.

target.title: fix(anthropic): bound streaming 200 success-body SSE reads at 16 MiB (provider path)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` (`iterateSseMessages`, the legacy provider SSE parser) accumulates an unbounded SSE byte stream into a string buffer while waiting for line delimiters. A hostile or malfunctioning Anthropic-compatible endpoint (including an MITM-able proxy or a hijacked self-hosted provider) can return a `Content-Length`-less SSE body that streams forever, exhausting process memory on a code path that runs for every legacy-provider Anthropic streaming call.

This is the streaming-body analogue of the bounded-read sweep Alix-007 finished for non-streaming `response.json()`. Same 16 MiB cap, same helper-driven design, but on the chunk-by-chunk SSE path that the non-streaming sweep did not cover.

## Changes

- `src/agents/streaming-byte-guard.ts` (NEW, 87 lines, copied from #96701 branch) — `createSseByteGuard` helper reused from the anthropic-transport-stream PR. Same minimal core-internal helper.
- `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts:347` — `iterateSseMessages` wraps `body.getReader()` in `createSseByteGuard` with `ANTHROPIC_MESSAGES_SUCCESS_BODY_MAX_BYTES = 16 * 1024 * 1024`. The existing SSE frame parsing loop and signal abort checks are unchanged. The function is also re-exported as `iterateSseMessagesForTest` for direct test access (mirrors the `parseAnthropicSseBodyForTest` pattern from #96701).

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: unbounded buffering of the Anthropic provider's `iterateSseMessages` SSE read; after the fix the read is capped at 16 MiB and the stream is cancelled on overflow.
- **Real environment tested**: a real `node:http` server bound to `127.0.0.1` returning a `Content-Length`-less 64 MiB body (4× the cap) chunk-by-chunk with backpressure, plus a small (8 MiB) negative-control body, plus a happy-path body. Drives the production `iterateSseMessagesForTest` over a real TCP socket. Node v22.22.0.
- **Exact steps**: `node --import tsx _proof_anthropic_provider_stream.mts` (proof script kept local-only, not committed, matching Alix-007 #96607 pattern).
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  === Anthropic provider streaming success-body bounded read (cap=16777216, would-stream≈67108864) ===

  PASS  Anthropic provider streaming success body bounded: rejected with "Anthropic Messages success body exceeded 16777216 bytes (received 16818468)..."; bytesSent=18874368 (< 67108864); server.aborted=true
  PASS  negative control: small body fully drained (8388608 bytes); cap did not trigger; aborted=false
  PASS  happy path: small valid Anthropic SSE response drained end-to-end (2 events); aborted=false

  === All Anthropic provider streaming success-body bounded-read assertions passed ===
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: `createSseByteGuard` cancelled the upstream `ReadableStreamDefaultReader` at ~16 MiB (well under 64 MiB). Server-side `bytesSent` observed at 18,874,368 — bounded, not drained. `cancel(reason)` received an `Error` instance. Negative control confirms the cap is the cause of overflow, not body structure.
- **What was not tested**: live Anthropic API call (the proof exercises the same production helper against the same shape of attack the live endpoint could carry); cross-platform Node 18/20 differences (Node 22 only, matches CI).

## Out of scope (separate PRs, sequenced)

- `src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.ts` (`parseSSE`, ChatGPT Responses SSE parser) — same bounded-read pattern, separate PR.
- `src/agents/runtime/proxy.ts` (`streamProxy`, internal proxy SSE parser) — same.
- `extensions/google/transport-stream.ts` and `extensions/ollama/src/{setup,stream}.ts` — need the helper promoted to a plugin-SDK subpath first.

## Risk

- **Low**: same 16 MiB cap already used by `readProviderJsonResponse` for non-streaming Anthropic success bodies. The behavior for normal-sized responses is unchanged — only the unbounded case now throws a clear overflow error instead of buffering without limit.
- **Cancel propagation**: `createSseByteGuard.cancel(reason)` propagates the overflow error to the underlying reader; verified by `expect(cancelReason).toBeInstanceOf(Error)` in the test.
- **No SDK surface change**: helper is core-internal (`src/agents/streaming-byte-guard.ts`); no `package.json` export, no `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json` entry, no API-baseline regen needed.
- **Reuses the helper from #96701**: same `createSseByteGuard` signature, same 16 MiB cap, same overflow-error shape. No new abstraction introduced.

## Diff stats

```
3 files changed, 146 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)
create mode 100644 src/agents/streaming-byte-guard.ts
```

Code change (excluding the inline test file): **108 LoC** (87 helper + 21 source). Test additions: 38 LoC inline in existing describe block.
target.author: wangmiao0668000666
